chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 796 - وَقَالَ زُرَارَةُ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِيَّاكَ وَ اِلْتِحَافَ اَلصَّمَّاءِ» قَالَ قُلْتُ وَ مَا اَلصَّمَّاءُ قَالَ «أَنْ تُدْخِلَ اَلثَّوْبَ مِنْ تَحْتِ جَنَاحِكَ فَتَجْعَلَهُ عَلَى مَنْكِبٍ وَاحِدٍ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.796 – Zurarah narrated that Abu Ja'far, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Beware of the 'wrapped shroud' (al-samma')."
I asked: "And what is the 'wrapped shroud'?"
Imam (as) said: "It is when you take your garment under your arm and place it over one shoulder”.
